text: Reconstructed the history with broker Beth Gittleman (Compass / The Catalyst Team) around selling apartment 6G at 300 W 145th, searching Gmail to build a timeline that pinned the "big disagreement" to the March 25â€“26 "Timeline and Strategy" thread (marketing/staging friction where Beth insisted price was the only lever) rather than a same-day email, which never surfaced in the index. Drafted a termination notice to Beth in the user's voice â€” then rewrote it to anchor explicitly on the pre-offer marketing impasse and keep a gracious tone, without sending or creating anything. When the user flagged the legal angle, read the signed Co-Op Exclusive Right to Sell listing agreement (term 12/28/2025â€“12/31/2026, ~6 months in) and confirmed his instinct: it is an exclusive right-to-sell with no early-termination clause, modifiable only by mutual written signed consent, with binding AAA arbitration and prevailing-party attorneys' fees. Bottom line reached: he cannot unilaterally terminate and any exit needs Compass's cooperation, so the letter should be framed as a decision made within that legal framework rather than a unilateral cancellation.
